home.heroDescription: Calculation tools, simulators and practical reference for real estate professionals in Luxembourg. Rent caps, acquisition fees, capital gains, state subsidies — all in one place.
home.missingToolDescription: tevaxia.lu is under active development. If you need a specific calculator, a feature for your professional practice, or if you find an error in our calculations — let us know.

home.heroDescription: Simulators dedicated to the energy transition of Luxembourg real estate. Measure the impact, estimate the ROI, explore energy communities.
home.impactDesc: Quantify the green premium / brown discount for each energy class (A to I). Comparative table, methodology and sources.
home.renovationDesc: Costs per work item, Klimabonus (25-62.5%), Klimaprêt, energy savings, NPV, IRR and payback period.
home.communauteDesc: Shared PV production, installation cost, savings per participant, monthly output, CO₂ avoided and regulatory compliance checklist.
home.epbdDesc: European Energy Performance of Buildings Directive. Key deadlines 2026-2050, stranding risk by class, long-term value impact.
home.estimateurDesc: Don't know your energy class? Answer 6 questions to get an indicative estimate of your energy passport rating.
home.lenozDesc: Assess your property's sustainability across the 6 categories of Luxembourg's LENOZ certification. Bronze to Platinum rating.
home.portfolioDesc: Analyse the energy profile of your property portfolio. Multi-property comparison, EPBD risk, weighted average score.
home.whyDesc: In Luxembourg, a property's energy class directly influences its market value. The gap between an A-rated and an I-rated property can reach {pct}. With the EPBD directive and rising energy costs, renovation is no longer optional — it's a measurable value driver.

impact.title: Energy class impact on property value
impact.description: Visualize how energy performance influences property prices in Luxembourg.
impact.source: Source: Observatoire de l'Habitat, Spuerkeess analyses
impact.apiConnecting: Connecting to calculation server... First load may take up to 60 seconds.
impact.localFallback: Local calculation — Spring Boot API not connected
impact.explainTitle: What is the green premium / brown discount?
impact.explainGreenText: Energy-efficient properties (classes A to C) sell above the market average. This premium reflects lower heating costs, superior thermal comfort and early compliance with future EPBD standards. In Luxembourg, Observatoire de l'Habitat studies show a premium of up to +8% for A-rated properties relative to the D reference class.
impact.explainBrownText: Conversely, energy-inefficient properties (classes E to I) suffer a discount. Buyers anticipate future renovation costs, rising energy prices and increasing regulatory constraints (progressive rental bans on energy-wasting buildings, EPBD directive 2024). The discount can reach -25% for I-rated properties.
impact.explainMethodText: Adjustment percentages are calibrated from observed transaction price gaps in Luxembourg, segmented by energy passport class. Class D serves as the reference (0% adjustment). Values are consistent with European academic studies and ECB climate risk analyses.
impact.explainSource1: Observatoire de l'Habitat du Luxembourg — Sales price analysis by energy class (2023-2025)
impact.explainSource2: ECB — Climate risk and real estate: emerging evidence on pricing (2024)
impact.explainSource3: EPBD recast Directive 2024/1275 — Zero-emission building stock by 2050
impact.explainSource4: TEGOVA EVS 2025 — ESG & Sustainability in Property Valuation (EVS 5)

renovation.title: Energy Renovation ROI
renovation.description: Estimate the cost, value gain and return on investment of an energy renovation.
renovation.aucunPoste: No renovation items identified for this class upgrade.
renovation.vanDesc: Net present value (3% discount rate, 3%/year energy price increase)
renovation.apiConnecting: Connecting to calculation server... First load may take up to 60 seconds.
renovation.localFallback: Local calculation — Spring Boot API not connected
renovation.roiDesc: Value gain / net cost after subsidies
renovation.klimapretEcoDesc: Klimaprêt payment: {mens} €/mo vs {mensMarche} €/mo at market rate
renovation.klimapretZeroCash: Zero upfront cash — Klimaprêt finances the full remaining cost

communaute.title: Energy Community Simulator
communaute.description: Estimate the savings and benefits of a renewable energy community between neighbours or co-owners.
communaute.paramAutoconso: Base self-consumption rate: {base}% + {foisonnement}% per participant (diversity effect)
communaute.paramCO2: CO₂ factor Luxembourg electricity mix: {val} g/kWh
communaute.coutBase: Based on €1,200/kWp ex-VAT (PV modules, inverter, wiring, installation). Luxembourg market 2025.
communaute.prodMensDesc: Distribution based on average solar irradiance in Luxembourg (south-facing, 35° tilt)
communaute.apiConnecting: Connecting to calculation server... First load may take up to 60 seconds.
communaute.localFallback: Local calculation — Spring Boot API not connected

epbd.title: EPBD Directive — Timeline & Stranding Risk
epbd.description: The European Energy Performance of Buildings Directive (EPBD recast 2024) sets a decarbonisation timeline for the building stock. Select your property's energy class to see the deadlines that apply and assess your stranding risk.
epbd.selectorLabel: Current energy class of your property
epbd.projectionGreenPremium: Green premium projection if class remains unchanged.
epbd.projectionBrownDiscount: Brown discount projection if no renovation is undertaken.
epbd.projectionDisclaimer: Indicative projections based on observed trends and the EPBD timeline. Not financial advice.
epbd.renovationCtaDescription: Estimate the cost, subsidies (Klimabonus, Klimaprêt) and return on investment of an energy renovation.
epbd.source1: Directive (EU) 2024/1275 of 24 April 2024 on the energy performance of buildings (recast)
epbd.source2: Official Journal of the European Union, L 2024/1275, 8 May 2024
epbd.source3: European Commission — Fit for 55, Renovation Wave Strategy
epbd.source4: Ministry of Energy and Spatial Planning, Luxembourg
epbd.milestone2026Detail: Deadline: 29 May 2026. Each member state must transpose the directive into national law.
epbd.milestone2028Detail: All new public buildings must be zero-emission from 2028.
epbd.milestone2030Detail: 16% reduction in primary energy consumption of the worst performing residential buildings.
epbd.milestone2033Detail: 26% reduction in primary energy consumption. Classes E-I must have undergone significant renovations.
epbd.milestone2035Detail: Zero-emission requirement for all new buildings (public and private).
epbd.milestone2040Detail: Total ban on fossil fuel boilers (gas, oil).
epbd.milestone2050Detail: Target: the entire European building stock reaches net zero emissions.

riskLow.description: Your property complies with 2050 targets. Value preserved or enhanced (green premium).
riskLow.action2: Consider improvements to maximise green premium
riskLow.valueImpact: Stable to positive. Growing green premium (+2 to +8% vs class D).

riskModerate.description: Class D: not a 'worst performer', but affected by the fossil boiler ban (2040) and zero-emission target (2050).
riskModerate.action1: Plan a renovation to class B or A before 2035
riskModerate.action2: Replace fossil boiler with a heat pump
riskModerate.action3: Take advantage of Klimabonus while subsidies are available
riskModerate.action4: Anticipate growing discount on mid-range classes
riskModerate.valueImpact: Progressive discount expected. By 2040, -8 to -12% without renovation.

riskHigh.description: Classes E-F: directly affected by the 2030-2033 reduction targets. Risk of non-compliance and significant discount.
riskHigh.action1: Start a deep energy renovation promptly
riskHigh.action2: Target at least class C, ideally B or A
riskHigh.action4: Maximise Klimabonus subsidies (higher rates for bigger class jumps)
riskHigh.valueImpact: Significant discount. By 2033, -10 to -18% without renovation.

riskCritical.description: Classes G-I: 'worst performer' impacted from 2030. Discount already visible and accelerating.
riskCritical.action2: Target class B or A to maximise ROI
riskCritical.action6: If selling: expect aggressive buyer negotiation
riskCritical.valueImpact: Critical discount. Current brown discount -12 to -25%. By 2030, potentially > -30%.

estimateurCpe.title: Estimate your energy class
estimateurCpe.description: Answer 6 simple questions to get an indicative estimate of the energy class (CPE) of your property in Luxembourg.
estimateurCpe.selectInstruction: Select the answer that best matches your property.
estimateurCpe.estimatedClassLabel: Estimated energy class of your property
estimateurCpe.disclaimerText: Only an official energy passport (CPE) issued by an accredited expert is legally binding.
estimateurCpe.exploreTitle: Explore simulators with your estimated class
estimateurCpe.exploreDescription: Class {classe} automatically selected in each simulator.

lenoz.title: Simplified LENOZ Scoring
lenoz.description: LENOZ is Luxembourg's sustainability certification for residential buildings. The official version includes 143 criteria across 6 categories. This simulator offers a simplified grid of approximately 20 key criteria for a quick pre-assessment.
lenoz.disclaimerText: Simplified estimate — the official LENOZ certification requires a full audit by an accredited body (143 criteria). This simulator provides a preliminary indication and does not replace the official process.

portfolio.title: Energy Portfolio
portfolio.subtitle: Analyse the energy profile of your property portfolio
portfolio.emptyDescription: Add your first property to start analysing the energy profile of your portfolio.
portfolio.ifAllClassB: If the entire portfolio reaches class B
portfolio.epbdWarning: {worst} of {total} properties are worst performers and must be renovated before 2033
portfolio.epbdDescription: The EPBD directive requires renovation of the least performing buildings (classes F, G, H, I) before 2033.

estimation.title: Property Estimation
estimation.subtitle: Data-driven estimation using Luxembourg public data
estimation.neufHint: VEFA reference price instead of existing
estimation.comparaisonExplication: Transactions reflect prices actually paid (notarial deeds). Listings reflect asking prices (often higher). The gap is an indicator of the negotiation margin.
estimation.margeNegociationDetail: Listed prices are on average {pct}% higher than prices actually paid (notarial deeds) in this municipality. This gap indicates the possible negotiation margin.
estimation.chargesExplication: Co-ownership charges ~€250/month for an apartment in Luxembourg. Property tax very low in LU (~€15/month).
estimation.renovationDisclaimer: Indicative ranges — Luxembourg market. Eligible for Klimabonus (up to 62.5%).
estimation.disclaimer: Indicative estimate based on public data from the Observatoire de l'Habitat and statistical adjustment coefficients. This is not a professional valuation. For an EVS 2025 compliant appraisal, use the
estimation.selectCommune: Select a municipality to get an estimate

fraisAcquisition.title: Acquisition Fees Simulator
fraisAcquisition.subtitle: Registration duties, Bëllegen Akt, VAT, notarial fees, mortgage fees
fraisAcquisition.residencePrincipaleHint: Eligible for Bëllegen Akt and 3% VAT
fraisAcquisition.infobellegenText: Tax credit of €40,000 per buyer (€80,000 for a couple) on registration duties. Only applicable for main residence and first use.
fraisAcquisition.infoVefaText: The 7% duties only apply to the land portion. The construction portion is subject to VAT (3% main residence, 17% otherwise), with a tax benefit cap of €50,000.
fraisAcquisition.infoNotaireText: Calculated according to a regulated degressive scale (4% up to €10,000 then degressive).

plusValues.title: Capital Gains Tax Calculator
plusValues.subtitle: Art. 99ter (speculation) and Art. 99bis / 102 LIR — STATEC revaluation coefficients
plusValues.legendeCession: > 2 years: Disposal — half rate + STATEC revaluation
plusValues.infoSpeculationText: Taxed at the full rate (progressive scale). No revaluation of the acquisition price.
plusValues.infoCessionText: Acquisition price revalued using STATEC coefficients. Decennial allowance of €50,000 (€100,000 for couples). Taxed at the half rate.
plusValues.infoResidenceText: Full exemption if effectively and continuously occupied.

calculLoyer.title: Rent Calculator — Invested Capital
calculLoyer.subtitle: Calculate the maximum legal rent under the law of 21 September 2006 on residential leases in Luxembourg.
calculLoyer.prixAcquisitionHint: Purchase price as stated in the notarial deed
calculLoyer.appliquerVetusteHint: Reduces invested capital based on the property age
calculLoyer.vetusteWarningText: Depreciation is not provided for by the 2006 law but may be invoked by the tenant. Application is at the discretion of the justice of the peace.
calculLoyer.loi2006Text: The annual rent may not exceed 5% of the invested capital revalued using STATEC revaluation coefficients.
calculLoyer.capitalInvestiText: revalued acquisition price + revalued improvement works.
calculLoyer.reforme2024Text: The threshold was lowered from 5% to 3.5% for dwellings whose invested capital exceeds a certain ceiling. Please check the applicable conditions.

achatLocation.title: Buy or Rent in Luxembourg
achatLocation.subtitle: Compare the evolution of your net wealth depending on whether you buy or rent your primary residence.
achatLocation.fraisAcquisitionHint: Registration duties, notary fees, VAT as applicable
achatLocation.entretienAnnuelHint: Annual maintenance budget (1% of price is a standard estimate)
achatLocation.tauxAssuranceSRDHint: Mandatory in Luxembourg. Rate between 0.15% and 0.40% of borrowed capital.
achatLocation.deductionInteretsDescription: Art. 98bis LIR: mortgage interest is deductible from taxable income (ceiling per person and per bracket).
achatLocation.tauxMarginalHint: Marginal income tax bracket. Class 1/1a: up to 42%, class 2: joint schedule.
achatLocation.indexationLoyerHint: Estimated annual rent increase (LU health index)
achatLocation.rendementPlacementHint: If you rent, the down payment is invested. Expected annual return.
achatLocation.verdictAchat: Over {horizon} years, buying is more advantageous
achatLocation.verdictLocation: Over {horizon} years, renting is more advantageous
achatLocation.croisementMessage: Buying becomes more advantageous from year {annee}
achatLocation.chartDescription: Net wealth from purchase (property value − outstanding capital) vs accumulated capital from renting (investment of down payment + cost differences).
achatLocation.hypothesesText: Property appreciation: {appreciation}%/yr. Investment return: {rendement}%/yr. Interest deduction ceiling: {plafond}/yr ({nbPersonnes} pers.). Balance insurance: {tauxSRD}% of capital.

simulateurAides.title: Housing Assistance Simulator
simulateurAides.subtitle: Estimate the state, municipal and tax benefits you may be entitled to for your real estate project in Luxembourg.
simulateurAides.montantTravauxHint: Total budget for energy renovation works
simulateurAides.bienNeufHint: Entitles to a tax credit of €20,000 per person
simulateurAides.epargneReguliereHint: Entitles to the housing savings premium and interest bonus
simulateurAides.residenceRequiseText: Most Luxembourg subsidies are conditional on occupancy as primary residence. For rental investments, only mortgage interest deductions and certain depreciations apply.
simulateurAides.estimationsText: The amounts displayed are estimates based on current schedules. The exact amount depends on your personal situation and detailed eligibility conditions.
simulateurAides.aidesCommunalesText: Aid varies considerably from one municipality to another. Here are some examples:
simulateurAides.communeLuxVille: Acquisition premium up to €30,000, rental assistance
simulateurAides.communeBeckerich: Energy model municipality — specific Klimabonus+ premiums
simulateurAides.contactCommune: Contact your municipal administration to find out the exact aid you may be entitled to.
simulateurAides.cumulText: State and municipal aid can generally be combined, up to an overall ceiling (as a rule, the total cannot exceed the total cost of the project).

dcfMulti.title: Multi-Tenant DCF
dcfMulti.subtitle: Discounted cash flow valuation for a multi-let investment property.
dcfMulti.capexHint: Recurring capital expenditure (major maintenance, equipment replacement)
dcfMulti.detteHint: For the equity IRR calculation (levered IRR)
dcfMulti.optionBreakHint: Leave empty if no break option
dcfMulti.ervHint: Estimated Rental Value — market rent per m²

carte.title: Property Price Map
carte.subtitle: Sale prices per m² by municipality in Luxembourg — sources: Land Registry, STATEC, listings.
carte.mapHint: Click on a municipality to view details.
carte.demographicsSource: Source: STATEC, National Register of Natural Persons
carte.clickCommune: Click on a municipality to display price details and demographics.

outilsBancaires.title: Banking Tools
outilsBancaires.subtitle: LTV, borrowing capacity, amortisation schedule and DSCR — compliant with Luxembourg banking practices (CRR, CSSF, EBA).
outilsBancaires.ltvStandardDesc: Standard LTV for a mortgage loan in Luxembourg. Required by most banks.
outilsBancaires.ltvFirstBuyerDesc: LTV tolerated for first-time buyers subject to conditions (sufficient income, balance insurance).
outilsBancaires.ltvStateGuaranteeDesc: Beyond 90%, a state guarantee or additional deposit is required.
outilsBancaires.prudentValueIntro: Luxembourg banks use the Mortgage Lending Value (MLV) pursuant to Art. 4(1)(74) CRR and EBA/GL/2020/06 guidelines.
outilsBancaires.prudentValueMethod: The MLV applies prudential discounts (cyclical, marketability, specific) to the market value to obtain a sustainable value.
outilsBancaires.prudentValueLegal: The CSSF requires the LTV ratio to be calculated on the MLV, not on the market value.
outilsBancaires.prudentValueCalculate: Calculate the MLV of your property with our <link>EVS 2025 valuation tool</link>.
outilsBancaires.remainingBalanceInsuranceHint: Mandatory in Luxembourg. Monthly cost added to the payment.
outilsBancaires.indicativeRatesSource: Indicative rates — Spuerkeess, BIL, Raiffeisen, BGL BNP Paribas. March 2025.
outilsBancaires.annualOperatingChargesHint: Non-recoverable landlord charges, maintenance, management, insurance, property tax
outilsBancaires.annualDebtServiceHint: Principal + interest repaid over the year
outilsBancaires.dscrBelow1Desc: Property does not cover the debt service
outilsBancaires.dscr1to1_2Desc: Low margin — risk in case of vacancy
outilsBancaires.dscrAbove1_2Desc: Minimum threshold required by most LU banks

bilanPromoteur.title: Developer Feasibility
bilanPromoteur.subtitle: Residual land value and feasibility analysis for a real estate development in Luxembourg.
bilanPromoteur.sellingPricePerSqmHint: Average selling price per sellable m²
bilanPromoteur.grossAreaHint: Gross external area (GEA) or floor area
bilanPromoteur.constructionCostPerSqmHint: Works cost per gross m² (€2,500–3,500/m² in Luxembourg)
bilanPromoteur.lotCountHint: Number of lots created by the subdivision
bilanPromoteur.preCommercialisationHint: Share of revenue pre-sold under VEFA before works start
bilanPromoteur.marginOnCAHint: Developer gross margin (10–20% of revenue in Luxembourg)
bilanPromoteur.notProfitableWithLand: The operation is not profitable at this land cost.
bilanPromoteur.negativeLandCharge: The land charge is negative: the project is not viable under these conditions.
bilanPromoteur.methodNote: The developer feasibility is a decision-support tool. Amounts are estimates and do not replace a detailed feasibility study by a construction economist.
bilanPromoteur.treasuryPlanDesc: Quarterly cash flow estimate with {pct}% VEFA pre-sales.
bilanPromoteur.noFinancingNeed: The project is self-financing through VEFA pre-sales.

marche.title: Market Database
marche.subtitle: Real estate market data in Luxembourg — residential, offices, retail, logistics, land and macro indicators.

valorisation.pageSubtitle: Value a property according to TEGOVA EVS 2025 standards: comparison, capitalisation, term & reversion, DCF, ESG, energy, MLV, reconciliation.
valorisation.refMarcheSource: Sources: Land Registry, STATEC, Housing Observatory, listings
valorisation.quartiersSourceNote: Indicative neighbourhood prices — sources: listings, transactions, local estimates.
valorisation.ajustezCaracteristiques: Adjust according to your property's characteristics.
valorisation.compCreePrixMoyen: A comparable will be created with the municipal average price. Then adjust the parameters.
valorisation.comparablesSub: Enter comparable sales and adjust characteristics.
valorisation.ajustementsHint: in % — positive = subject property is superior
valorisation.selectCommuneStart: Search a municipality to start the valuation
valorisation.loyerMarcheERVHint: If provided, enables reversionary yield calculation
valorisation.capTauxCapNote: The capitalisation rate reflects the market's expected return. A low rate = high value. Adjust based on segment, location and risk.
valorisation.tauxActualisationHint: WACC or investor's required rate of return
valorisation.tauxCapSortieHint: Cap rate applied at resale at end of period
valorisation.fraisCessionHint: Transfer duties, agent commission (6–8% in Luxembourg)
valorisation.dcfTauxNote: The discount rate and exit yield are the most sensitive DCF parameters. Calibrate them with recent market transactions.
valorisation.dcfSensibiliteNote: The highlighted cell corresponds to the selected parameters.
valorisation.trERVHint: Estimated Rental Value — annual market rent
valorisation.trTauxReversionHint: Yield on market rent — less secure, therefore higher
valorisation.trMethodeNote: The term & reversion method values secured income (term) and future market income (reversion) separately.
valorisation.esgEVSNote: ESG assessment compliant with EVS 5 (TEGOVA EVS 2025) — Sustainability in Property Valuation.
valorisation.enValeurApresHint: Estimated property value once target class is reached
valorisation.enFourchetteNote: Estimate based on Luxembourg statistical data.
valorisation.enTravauxHint: Total amount of energy renovation works
valorisation.enMargePrudentielleHint: Safety margin on total cost (5–15% recommended)
valorisation.enPrincipeText: The residual value after energy renovation is the improved property value minus net works cost and a prudential margin.
valorisation.enFormule: Value after renovation − Net costs − Prudential margin
valorisation.enArt208EBAText: For mortgage lending, the lending value (MLV) must reflect the sustainable value of the property.
valorisation.mlvValeurMarcheEVS1: Market value per EVS 1 (TEGOVA). Derived from the reconciliation of valuation methods.
valorisation.mlvIssueReconciliation: The market value is automatically taken from the Reconciliation tab.
valorisation.mlvCRRNote: The MLV is defined by Art. 4(1)(74) CRR and EBA/GL/2020/06 guidelines.
valorisation.mlvCRR2Subtitle: Regulatory risk weights by loan-to-value ratio (LTV)
valorisation.recScenarioHautHint: Upward variation applied to the reconciled value
valorisation.recScenarioBasHint: Downward variation applied to the reconciled value
valorisation.recScenarioNote: Scenarios allow testing the sensitivity of the value.
valorisation.recScenariosNote: High and low scenarios present a value range in line with TEGOVA EVS 2025 best practice.
